hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The first line of output is an integer representing the number of items where completed is true.
A single line string indicating the input is invalid, following the delimiter rules of the application (usually a newline).

trigger_keywords: test cases for feature2 2 filter completed
show me feature2 2 filter completed test cases
what are the feature2 2 filter completed inputs and outputs
give me the feature2_2_filter_completed test file

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_construct_task.json.description: Create a single task from its text. When the completion flag is omitted it defaults to not-completed; when supplied it is honored verbatim. The output echoes the stored text and the resolved completion state.

feature1_2_serialize_task.json.description: Round-trip a task through its JSON map representation: decode a map with text and completion keys into a task, then re-encode it. The decoded text and flag are reported, plus the canonical re-encoded JSON which must be value-equal to the original map.

feature1_3_toggle_task.json.description: Toggle a task's completion flag a number of times starting from a known initial state. Each toggle flips the flag, so the reported sequence of states must strictly alternate starting from the initial value.

feature2_1_add_tasks.json.description: Append a sequence of task texts to an initially empty list. The list grows by one per append and preserves insertion order. The output reports the final size followed by each stored text in order.

feature2_2_filter_completed.json.description: From a mixed list of completed and not-completed tasks, select only the completed ones while preserving their original relative order. The output reports the number of completed tasks followed by their texts in order.

feature2_3_persist_roundtrip.json.description: Persist a list of tasks to durable key-value storage, then reload them into a fresh, empty list. The reloaded list must contain exactly the same tasks, in order, with text and completion state preserved through the save/load round-trip.

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct' and fields are 'text' and optionally 'completed'
_constraints.output_rule: Echo text and resolved completion state separated by newline

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct' and 'completed' key is omitted
_constraints.output_rule: Output completion state as false

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct' and 'completed' key is supplied
_constraints.output_rule: Output completion state verbatim from input

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'serialize' with input object containing 'text' and 'completed'
_constraints.output_rule: Output text, completed, and JSON representation of input on separate lines
JSON output must be a single-line map with keys 'text' and 'completed', double-quoted strings
Output initial state, then state after each toggle on separate lines
State toggles from input; sequence must strictly alternate (f->t->f or t->f->t)

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'add' with array of strings 'tasks'
_constraints.output_rule: Output final count followed by numbered items (1-based index) on separate lines
Items must be printed in original insertion order with increasing numeric index

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'filter_completed' with array of task objects
_constraints.output_rule: Output count of completed items followed by filtered items in relative original order
Only items where 'completed' is true are included in the result list
Item format is 'index=original_text'

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'persist' with array of task objects
_constraints.output_rule: Output reloaded count and reloaded items in 'text|completed' format
Format: text string directly followed by pipe '|', then boolean completion state (no space)
Order of items on output must match order of input array
All state (text and boolean) must survive the persistent store round-trip exactly

_constraints.input_condition: command is malformed or missing required 'mode'
_constraints.output_rule: Output a neutral error line (delimited by newline)
Stdout must contain ONLY raw program output lines (no logs, pass/fail, or metadata)
Output file name pattern: `{filename.stem}@{case_index.zfill(3)}.txt`
Output must be namespaced by `<cases-dir>` path

hide.replacement: the total count of matching items
hide.oracle_response: The first line of output is an integer representing the number of items where completed is true.

hide.replacement: an appropriate error message for malformed input
hide.oracle_response: A single line string indicating the input is invalid, following the delimiter rules of the application (usually a newline).
